title: Cleisostoma koeteiense
intro: Cleisostoma koeteiense, also known as Koetei's Cleisostoma, is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation and fever, due to its reported health benefits of reducing oxidative stress and improving immune function. The bioactive constituents of Cleisostoma koeteiense include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cleisostoma koeteiense is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the production of herbal teas, tinctures, and extracts.
taxonomy: Cleisostoma koeteiense is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Cleisostoma, which consists of several species of orchids found in tropical regions. Cleisostoma koeteiense is further classified as a member of the subtribe Aeridinae, within the tribe Vandeae. The species is native to the tropical regions of New Guinea. This classification is based on the plant's morphology, anatomy, and genetic characteristics.
